title: Global warming from 2015 to 2030: Interactive visualization maps of trends and regional differences
description: This project is a highly interactive global warming visualization platform built on D3.js, designed to dynamically display global surface temperature trends and regional variations from 2015 to 2030. Through a pan-zoomable map heatmap and time-synchronized national line charts, it enables users to freely switch between raw temperature data and anomaly patterns, facilitating intuitive and detailed analysis of the scale and pace of climate change. Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

title: Geospatial visualizations - bikewatching
description: An experimental project using Mapbox GL JS to create interactive maps and visualize bicycle lanes, bike stations, and traffic flow data. The project also covers building data filtering tools (such as sliders), implementing responsiveness, and performing performance optimizations to ensure the map's usability and smooth operation.

title: Dark Data: Exploring Power Outage Patterns
description: Investigated the impact of climate and price on electricity outages using hypothesis testing and a Random Forest Regression model (built with Python/Pandas) to reduce prediction error by 7% and published the actionable results on an interactive webpage for grid resilience policy.

title: Series/DataFrame Implementation Project
description: Engineered an object-oriented data manipulation framework in Java, building BST Series and hash table-backed DataFrames using custom data structures (linked lists, BSTs, hash tables) and generics to accelerate Series search from O(n) to O(log n) and enable constant-time DataFrame column retrieval.

title: Taylor Swift Music Analytics & Recommender
description: Constructed a music analytics and recommendation platform using Python, babypandas, and NumPy to explore over 200 songs from Spotify and Genius datasets, merging data, engineering Euclidean distance similarity metrics, and generating visualization dashboards to enhance music discovery and data insights.

title: Friends Sitcom Reboot: Strategic Data Analysis
description: This project supported a reboot strategy by using exploratory data analysis, statistical inference, and bootstrapped estimates to assess 236 episodes, analyzing IMDb scores, dialogue sentiment, and gender distribution to inform casting and narrative decisions and identify top-performing creative contributors and equity gaps.
